TextENGNew: April 30th, 1982.@rTo the director of the Gogura University Hospital.@rThe application for the manufacture and sale of the following drugs has been denied.
1. Nomenclature.@rProduct title: Placil β.@r(Selective psychotransmitter reuptake inhibitor.)

Causes target to feel better and more motivated. Primarily for the treatment of depression and panic disorders, but in addition to those can be used to treat obsessive compulsive neurosis and eating disorders.

By being transported to the brain through the bloodstream, it inhibits the reuptake of psychotransmitters within the signal transduction nerves, and also increases the concentration of transmitters in the synaptic cleft of the brain.
As a result, neurotransmission ability is improved, which reduces the user's mental burden and calms their mood.
4. Reason for rejection.@rIn cases of overdose, symptoms such as severe drowsiness, confusion, hallucinations and, in some patients, self-inflicted harm were identified in late clinical trials.
Additionally, analysis of the active components is still difficult at the present time.
5. Et cetera.@rIf submitting another application, analysis of the components mentioned in item 4 and a description of the active effects will be required.
Ministry of Health and Welfare, Pharmaceutical and Medical Safety Bureau.
